mnemonics: Groups of Pirates Can't Attack (GPCA): Stands for Group Communication Protocols, Pragmatic General Multicast (PGM), Collaborative Applications, and Application Layer Multicast.
keyPoints: Group communication protocols like IGMP manage multicast group membership.
Reliable multicast protocols (e.g., PGM) ensure data delivery to all group members.
Application layer multicast is used where network layer support is unavailable.
commonMistakes: Confusing multicast (one-to-many) with unicast (one-to-one) or broadcast (one-to-all) communication.
Assuming all multicast protocols guarantee reliable delivery by default.
Overlooking the security implications of open group membership in multicast models.
